name: Ohio v. American Express Co.
question: Under the “rule of reason,” was the government’s showing that American Express’s anti-steering provisions stifled price competition on the merchant side of the credit-card platform sufficient to prove anti-competitive effects, thereby shifting to American Express the burden of establishing pro-competitive benefits from the provisions?
description: A case in which the Court held, under the “rule of reason,” that the government’s showing that American Express’s anti-steering provisions stifled price competition on the merchant side of the credit-card platform was not sufficient to prove a violation of antitrust law.
